title: Caregiver - View & Manage PwDs (End-to-End) - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the 'My PwDs' page on the AT/AD platform where caregivers view and manage PwDs under their care. Covers PwD limit display, slot tracking, PwD card information (name, contact, disability type, date added, View Profile CTA), Add New PwD button visibility, Add Another PwD card with remaining count, and edge cases including count mismatch sync, admin-removed PwD slot recovery, and session expiry redirect. Related stories SCRUM-375 (limit enforcement), SCRUM-378 (profile management), and SCRUM-381 (delete PwD) provide additional context.

inScope: My PwDs page display with PwD limit and slot tracking
PwD card content: Name, Contact details (mobile/email), Disability type, Date added, View Profile CTA
Add New PwD button visibility based on remaining slots
Add Another PwD card with remaining count display
Empty state when no PwDs are added
Multiple PwD cards display and layout
outOfScope: PwD profile view details (covered in SCRUM-378)
PwD limit enforcement logic (covered in SCRUM-375)

testCases.title: Verify My PwDs page displays PwD limit and slots used/remaining
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered, logged in, and has at least one PwD added
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with existing PwDs
2. Navigate to 'My PwDs' page
3. Verify the page loads successfully
4. Locate the PwD limit indicator (e.g., max 5)
5. Verify the total PwD limit is displayed
6. Verify the number of slots used is displayed
7. Verify the number of remaining slots is displayed
8. Verify the count matches the actual number of PwD cards shown
expectedResults: My PwDs page loads without errors
PwD limit is clearly displayed (e.g., '5 PwDs allowed')
Slots used count matches actual PwD cards on page
Remaining slots count is accurate (limit - used)
Limit and slot information is prominently visible
testCases.postconditions: My PwDs page is displayed with accurate limit and slot information

testCases.title: Verify each PwD card displays name, contact, disability type, date added, and View Profile CTA
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in with at least one PwD registered
steps: 1. Navigate to 'My PwDs' page
2. Locate the first PwD card
3. Verify PwD Name is displayed on the card
4. Verify Contact details are displayed (mobile number)
5. Verify Contact details are displayed (email address)
6. Verify Disability type is displayed
7. Verify Date added is displayed
8. Verify 'View Profile' CTA button/link is present
9. If multiple PwDs exist, verify all cards show the same information structure
10. Verify card layout is consistent across all PwD cards
expectedResults: PwD Name is clearly visible on the card
Mobile number is displayed in contact details
Email address is displayed in contact details
Disability type is shown with clear label or tag
Date added is displayed in readable format
'View Profile' CTA is visible and clickable
All PwD cards follow the same layout structure
testCases.postconditions: PwD card content is verified for completeness

testCases.title: Verify 'Add New PwD' button is visible when slots remain
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; PwD count is below the limit
steps: 1. Navigate to 'My PwDs' page
2. Verify current PwD count is below the limit
3. Locate the 'Add New PwD' button
4. Verify the button is visible and enabled
5. Click the 'Add New PwD' button
6. Verify user is navigated to PwD registration/add flow
7. Navigate back to My PwDs page
8. Verify the button is still visible
expectedResults: 'Add New PwD' button is visible when slots remain
Button is enabled and clickable
Clicking the button navigates to PwD add/registration flow
Button remains visible as long as slots are available
testCases.postconditions: Add New PwD button visibility and functionality verified

testCases.title: Verify 'Add Another PwD' card shows remaining slot count
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in with at least one PwD but below the limit
steps: 1. Navigate to 'My PwDs' page
2. Locate the 'Add Another PwD' card among the PwD cards
3. Verify the card displays the remaining slot count
4. Verify the remaining count is accurate (limit - current PwDs)
5. Add a new PwD (if possible)
6. Return to My PwDs page
7. Verify the remaining count has decreased by 1
8. Verify the card is visually distinct from PwD cards
expectedResults: 'Add Another PwD' card is visible among PwD cards
Remaining slot count is displayed (e.g., '3 slots remaining')
Count is accurate and matches (limit - used)
Count updates dynamically when PwDs are added
Card is visually distinct from PwD information cards
testCases.postconditions: Add Another PwD card with remaining count is verified

testCases.title: Verify View Profile CTA navigates to PwD profile details
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in with at least one PwD registered
steps: 1. Navigate to 'My PwDs' page
2. Locate a PwD card
3. Click the 'View Profile' CTA on the card
4. Verify user is navigated to the PwD profile details page
5. Verify the profile page displays the correct PwD information
6. Verify the PwD name on profile matches the card
7. Navigate back to My PwDs page
8. Verify My PwDs page loads correctly after returning
expectedResults: 'View Profile' CTA is clickable on each PwD card
Clicking navigates to the correct PwD's profile page
Profile page displays matching PwD information
Navigation back to My PwDs works correctly

testCases.title: Verify multiple PwD cards display correctly with different data
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has 3+ PwDs registered with different disability types and dates
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with multiple PwDs
2. Navigate to 'My PwDs' page
3. Verify all PwD cards are displayed
4. Verify each card shows unique PwD name
5. Verify each card shows correct contact details for that PwD
6. Verify each card shows the correct disability type
7. Verify each card shows the correct date added
8. Verify each card has its own 'View Profile' CTA
9. Verify cards are laid out consistently without overlap
10. Verify slot count matches the number of cards displayed
expectedResults: All PwD cards are displayed with correct individual data
Each card shows unique name, contact, disability type, and date
Each card has a functional View Profile CTA
Cards are laid out consistently without visual issues
Slot count matches number of cards
testCases.postconditions: Multiple PwD card display is verified

testCases.title: Verify PwD count mismatch is resolved by page refresh/sync
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is on My PwDs page; A PwD has been added or removed in another session/tab
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver and navigate to My PwDs page
2. Note the current PwD count and cards displayed
3. In a separate session/tab, add or remove a PwD (simulating concurrent change)
4. Return to the original My PwDs page
5. Refresh the page
6. Verify the PwD count updates to reflect the change
7. Verify the PwD cards match the updated count
8. Verify remaining slots are recalculated correctly
expectedResults: Page refresh syncs the PwD count with backend data
PwD cards match the updated count after refresh
Remaining slots are recalculated correctly
No stale data is displayed after refresh
testCases.postconditions: Count mismatch sync behavior is verified

testCases.title: Verify slot count updates dynamically after adding a new PwD
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in with available PwD slots
steps: 1. Navigate to My PwDs page
2. Note current PwD count and remaining slots (e.g., 2/5, 3 remaining)
3. Click 'Add New PwD' button
4. Complete the PwD registration flow
5. Return to My PwDs page
6. Verify PwD count has increased by 1 (e.g., 3/5)
7. Verify remaining slots decreased by 1 (e.g., 2 remaining)
8. Verify the new PwD card is displayed with correct information
9. Verify 'Add Another PwD' card shows updated remaining count
expectedResults: PwD count increases by 1 after adding a new PwD
Remaining slots decrease by 1
New PwD card appears with correct information
Add Another PwD card shows updated remaining count
All counts are accurate and consistent
testCases.postconditions: Dynamic slot update after PwD addition is verified

AC1_my_pwds_page_limit_slots.description: My PwDs page displays PwD limit, slots used and remaining

AC2_pwd_card_content.description: Each PwD card shows Name, Contact details, Disability type, Date added, View Profile CTA

AC3_add_new_pwd_button.description: Add New PwD button is visible if slots remain

AC4_add_another_pwd_card.description: Add Another PwD card shows remaining count

EC1_count_mismatch_sync.description: PwD count mismatch → refresh syncs data

assumptions: Caregiver registration and sign-in flows are functional (SCRUM-357, SCRUM-360)
PwD registration flow is functional for adding PwDs (SCRUM-372)
PwD limit is configured as 5 in QA environment
Multiple caregiver accounts with varying PwD counts are available
dependencies: SCRUM-357: Caregiver registration must be functional to create test accounts
SCRUM-360: Caregiver sign-in must work for accessing My PwDs page
SCRUM-372: PwD registration flow needed for adding PwDs during testing
SCRUM-375: PwD limit enforcement logic must be implemented
SCRUM-378: PwD profile view must be available for View Profile CTA testing
SCRUM-381: PwD deletion must be available for slot recovery testing

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What is the exact PwD limit per caregiver? Is it always 5 or configurable?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects all test cases involving limit and slot calculations

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is the 'Add Another PwD' card a separate visual element from the 'Add New PwD' button, or are they the same?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_MY_PWD_003 and TC_MY_PWD_004 - may be testing the same element

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What date format is used for 'Date added' on PwD cards?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_MY_PWD_002 expected date format verification

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Does the page auto-refresh to sync data, or does the user need to manually refresh?

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_MY_PWD_001-004) should be executed first - core page display and slot tracking
High priority tests (005-010) cover card interactions, limit states, empty state, multiple cards, sync, dynamic slot updates

estimated_effort.critical_path: 4-6 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: QA environment with AT/AD platform accessible
Multiple caregiver accounts with varying PwD counts (0, 2-3, 5)
PwD registration flow functional for adding PwDs

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria covered; edge cases for admin removal, session expiry, partial data, accessibility, max limit, and empty state removed
